mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month. The trend in INDPRO is a leading indicator of cyclical profits.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month change and forward 20-day return is less than 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% indicates labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or average forward return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation combined with low federal funds rates creates negative real rates, eroding purchasing power and signaling eventual tightening. This unsustainable combination predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Average forward 20-day return in triggered periods is not negative with 90% confidence, or the interaction coefficient in a linear model is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1%, the monetary environment is highly accommodative, compressing risk premiums and boosting equity valuations. This regime reliably produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: In instances where FEDFUNDS < 1%, average forward 20-day return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1500 thousand units signals a weakening housing sector, which is a leading indicator of economic contraction. This predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day cumulative return following triggers is not negative on average, or hit rate below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicate a robust consumption-driven economy, boosting corporate earnings and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rise > 100,000 AND RSAFS rise > 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Average forward 20-day return in triggered instances is not positive, or the interaction effect in a linear model is not significant at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of strong GDP growth (above 2.5% annualized) combined with contained inflation (CPI below 290), the economy is in a sweet spot: high growth without overheating. This environment drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Average forward 60-day return in this regime is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate strong consumer demand, which drives corporate revenues and earnings. This constant signal, independent of threshold, predicts positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month change with forward 5-day return is below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window.
